objective: Define governance package v0.2 as the SSOT bootstrap contract for thin downstream repo governance consumers, preserving repo-specific adapters for HIPAA, plan-mode, lane policy, local runtime, exempt knowledge, and product-baseline constraints.
scope_boundary: Governance package contract and planning artifacts only.
Extend the existing governance tooling package manifest instead of creating a second bootstrap mechanism.
Document profile inheritance, managed surfaces, and local override contracts.
Do not edit downstream product repositories in this slice.
out_of_scope: Deleting copied downstream hooks before managed parity is proven.
Weakening HealthcarePlatform HIPAA, PHI/PII, or lane policy requirements.
Implementing the v0.2 verifier or deployer expansion; that is issue #454.
research_summary: Preplanning compared the proposed governance SSOT plus thin repo adapter model against current governance package and downstream repo states. Research found an existing Local CI-centered package foundation with partial downstream adoption, duplicated local hooks across repos, mutable workflow refs in some products, and repo-specific constraints that must become typed profile extensions rather than copied prose.

sprints.name: Package v0.2 SSOT contract
sprints.goal: Make the package manifest describe shared governance surfaces beyond Local CI.
tasks: Add package v0.2 bootstrap contract metadata.
Add shared surface classes for hooks, workflows, lane policies, plan preflight, CLI session events, and handoff package lifecycle refs.
Add profile inheritance and local override requirements.
acceptance_criteria: The manifest states that v0.2 extends v0.1 rather than replacing it.
Managed surfaces cover hooks, reusable workflows, schemas, Local CI, lane policy, plan preflight, CLI supervision, and handoff refs.
Local overrides require issue, owner, reason, and review cadence or expiry.

sprints.name: Repo profile contract
sprints.goal: Preserve repo-specific controls through typed profiles instead of copied governance logic.
tasks: Define baseline and extension profile names.
Document HealthcarePlatform, Seek, LAM, ASC, and product-baseline constraints.
Document forbidden local overrides that weaken stricter profiles.
acceptance_criteria: HealthcarePlatform HIPAA/PII/lane constraints are represented as profile requirements.
Seek plan-mode and LAM local runtime constraints are represented as profile requirements.
ASC exemption and Stampede product-baseline behavior have explicit profile slots.

specialist_reviews.focus: Existing governance consumer package, managed-file contract, profiles, and downstream bootstrap foundations.
specialist_reviews.summary: Found a Local CI-centered package foundation suitable for extension to v0.2 rather than replacement.

specialist_reviews.focus: Current governance pointers, hooks, workflows, and local overrides across governed repos.
specialist_reviews.summary: Found partial adoption, copied hook drift, and repo-specific constraints requiring typed local profiles.

specialist_reviews.focus: PDCA/R rollout strategy for SSOT package plus thin local adapters.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended a v0.2 package contract followed by verifier expansion and per-repo rollout PRs.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 returned APPROVED_WITH_CHANGES. Required profile-key and issue #444 dependency clarifications were applied in the manifest and recorded in the cross-review artifact.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement issue #453 governance package v0.2 contract artifacts only; no downstream repo edits.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate JSON/schema surfaces, record validation, and obtain required cross-review before merge.

material_deviation_rules: If verifier behavior must change, stop and route to issue #454 unless the change is only manifest-compatible scaffolding.
If downstream repo files are required, stop and route to the relevant rollout child issue.
If HealthcarePlatform constraints cannot be represented without weakening them, stop and split an HP-specific profile design issue.
If cross-review is unavailable, do not mark the slice merge-ready.
approved_by: operator directive: follow repo rules and proceed without HITL
